row.cost_basis: freeside-sonar Railway dashboard 2026-06-15 (ADR §'verified cost reality'): $133.21 total = ~$58 productive + ~$25 dead-waste + ~$25 migration-tax + Postgres-RAM-dominated.
row.notes: AS-BILLED = today's full bill incl. ~$50/mo removable waste (bd-4kf cutover + real orphan cleanup recover it INDEPENDENT of the architecture decision). cost_source=measured refers to the $/mo (real invoice). toil INCIDENT COUNT measured (3 named); per-incident MINUTES estimated post-hoc.
row.scale_ceiling: Postgres RAM dominates (~$74/mo); caps→resizes as event density grows

row.cost_basis: ADR invoice decomposition: ~$58 productive-clean (vRR1 + green-v3 + belt-hasura-green + erpc + belt-gateway). The clean cost the stack holds at ONLY with continuous toil.
row.notes: PRODUCTIVE-CLEAN 1x baseline = what sovereign-Ponder costs without the removable waste; ≈ Envio's flat ~$70 BEFORE toil is priced. This is the head-to-head row vs managed at 1x.
row.scale_ceiling: Postgres RAM steps up with event density; the resize treadmill is the real ceiling

row.cost_basis: operator-recollected lived Envio bill ~$70/mo flat (ADR, lived-authoritative). CURRENT Envio pricing UNPUBLISHED / Discord-quote-gated (envio.dev/pricing + hosted-service-billing, retrieved 2026-06-16) — could NOT re-confirm. Tagged vendor-quote (not measured) because it is not reproducible now. Berachain FIRST-CLASS HyperSync (chain 80094) verified.
row.notes: MANAGED ⇒ 0 toil. STAND UP via the runbook to upgrade $70 → measured + confirm the current tier. The operator's whole thesis (Envio was ~$70 & zero-attention) rests on re-confirming this.
row.scale_ceiling: event-throughput tier (cost scales with event density, not contract count); exact $ gated behind a sales quote

row.cost_basis: DERIVED from published unit rates (goldsky.com/pricing, docs.goldsky.com/pricing/summary, retrieved 2026-06-16): subgraph compute ~$36.50/mo/worker + storage $4/100k entities. Point est ~$73/mo @ ~1M entities; RANGE $37–170/mo. The load-bearing unknown is ENTITY count (schema-dependent), NOT the 3.5M event count. Berachain dedicated partnership verified.
row.notes: MANAGED ⇒ 0 toil. Published rates are real; the $/mo is my derivation, not a Goldsky quote for this footprint. Free tier is a dev allowance, insufficient for prod.
row.scale_ceiling: entity count ($4/100k beyond 100k free) + Mirror records-written ($1/100k beyond 1M) — a high-cardinality schema inflates cost fast

row.cost_basis: PROJECTED: ponder($) ≈ 23 fixed + 35×2 = $93/mo (RAM scales ~linearly with event density; conservative upper bound). Toil scales with the resize treadmill (4 incidents/mo @ 60min). Anchored to the measured 1x productive-clean $58.
row.notes: Sovereign at 2x: the story is the TOIL curve, not the dollar curve. Directional only.
row.scale_ceiling: Postgres RAM cap → resize treadmill; the toil grows faster than the dollars

row.cost_basis: PROJECTED (DOUBLY uncertain): envio($) ≈ 70×2 = $140/mo, scaled from the UNVERIFIED ~$70 lived base × throughput. Envio pricing unpublished. Directional only.
row.notes: Managed at 2x: dollars may rise with throughput, but toil stays 0 — that gap is the whole finding.
row.scale_ceiling: usage tier steps up with throughput; still 0 operator toil

row.cost_basis: PROJECTED: ponder($) ≈ 23 fixed + 35×5 = $198/mo (RAM scales ~linearly with event density; conservative upper bound). Toil scales with the resize treadmill (6 incidents/mo @ 60min). Anchored to the measured 1x productive-clean $58.
row.notes: Sovereign at 5x: the story is the TOIL curve, not the dollar curve. Directional only.
row.scale_ceiling: Postgres RAM cap → resize treadmill; the toil grows faster than the dollars

row.cost_basis: PROJECTED (DOUBLY uncertain): envio($) ≈ 70×5 = $350/mo, scaled from the UNVERIFIED ~$70 lived base × throughput. Envio pricing unpublished. Directional only.
row.notes: Managed at 5x: dollars may rise with throughput, but toil stays 0 — that gap is the whole finding.
row.scale_ceiling: usage tier steps up with throughput; still 0 operator toil

row.cost_basis: ClickHouse Cloud storage $25.30/TB-mo (getbeton/pulse/improvado 2026-05) × ~100 B/row compressed (CryptoHouse 6.11× real on-chain). event_count MEASURED (Dune nft.transfers ethereum, exec 2026-06-16, query 7737010); $/mo PROJECTED = events × bytes/row × storage-rate. STORAGE LINE ONLY — compute/RAM is separate and is the larger serving cost (favors columnar).
row.notes: ClickHouse columnar arm. HyperSync extraction $0 marginal at POC scale (self-host + free/cheap token); production rate UNPUBLISHED (sales-quote). Query latency not measured (null).
row.scale_ceiling: Ethereum has only ~406k NFT collections / ~396M lifetime Transfer events (~40GB columnar) — 'millions of collections' does NOT exist on one chain; the firehose has no cost wall here. Registration's ceiling is OPERATIONAL (per-contract enumeration), not cost.

row.cost_basis: Railway Postgres volume $0.15/GB-mo (makerkit @railway/pricing 2026-05) × ~600 B/row (~6× columnar). event_count MEASURED (Dune nft.transfers ethereum, exec 2026-06-16, query 7737010); $/mo PROJECTED = events × bytes/row × storage-rate. STORAGE LINE ONLY — compute/RAM is separate and is the larger serving cost (favors columnar). The REAL Postgres cost at scale is RAM ($10/GB-mo) to hold a 200+GB row-store working set — NOT in this storage line.
row.notes: Postgres row-store arm. Storage line ~6× ClickHouse; the hidden cost is RAM-to-serve. Query latency not measured (null).
row.scale_ceiling: Ethereum has only ~406k NFT collections / ~396M lifetime Transfer events (~40GB columnar) — 'millions of collections' does NOT exist on one chain; the firehose has no cost wall here. Registration's ceiling is OPERATIONAL (per-contract enumeration), not cost.
